84	// readinessVerdict is the verdict reported for a single readiness check.
85	// Keep this coercion aligned with cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json
86	// so the shared producer prompt and this decoder accept the same payload shape.
87	// The intake producer may emit verdict as a JSON bool, a JSON string, or omit
88	// it entirely; UnmarshalJSON coerces all three forms to a canonical lowercased
89	// string so downstream fail-detection (failedReadinessReasons) can compare it
90	// deterministically:
91	//   - JSON bool true  -> "pass"
92	//   - JSON bool false -> "fail"
93	//   - JSON string     -> trimmed, lowercased value (for example "PASS" -> "pass")
94	//   - absent / null   -> "" (empty, same as a missing field)
95	type readinessVerdict string
96	
97	func (v *readinessVerdict) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
98		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
99		if trimmed == "" || trimmed == "null" {
100			*v = ""
101			return nil
102		}
103		switch trimmed[0] {
104		case 't', 'f':
105			var b bool
106			if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &b); err != nil {
107				return err
108			}
109			if b {
110				*v = "pass"
111			} else {
112				*v = "fail"
113			}
114			return nil
115		case '"':
116			var s string
117			if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &s); err != nil {
118				return err
119			}
120			*v = readinessVerdict(strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(s)))
121			return nil
122		default:
123			return fmt.Errorf("readiness_checks verdict must be a bool, string, or null, got %s", jsonKindName(trimmed[0]))
124		}
125	}
126	
127	func jsonKindName(first byte) string {
128		switch first {
129		case '{':
130			return "object"
131		case '[':
132			return "array"
133		case '"':
134			return "string"
135		case 't', 'f':
136			return "bool"
137		case 'n':
138			return "null"
139		default:
140			return "number"
141		}
142	}

160		if trimmed == "" || trimmed == "null" {
161			return nil
162		}

164		return nil

167	type preClaimReadinessSuggestedFixesPayload []preClaimReadinessSuggestedFix
168	
169	func (p *preClaimReadinessSuggestedFixesPayload) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
170		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
171		if trimmed == "" || trimmed == "null" {
172			return nil
173		}
174		if trimmed[0] != '[' {
175			return fmt.Errorf("suggested_fixes must be a JSON array")
176		}
177		var raw []json.RawMessage
178		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &raw); err != nil {
179			return err
180		}
181		fixes := make([]preClaimReadinessSuggestedFix, 0, len(raw))
182		for _, item := range raw {
183			itemTrimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(item))
184			if itemTrimmed == "" || itemTrimmed == "null" {
185				continue
186			}
187			switch itemTrimmed[0] {
188			case '"':
189				var fix string
190				if err := json.Unmarshal(item, &fix); err != nil {
191					return err
192				}
193				fixes = append(fixes, preClaimReadinessSuggestedFix{Fix: strings.TrimSpace(fix)})
194			case '{':
195				var fix preClaimReadinessSuggestedFix
196				if err := json.Unmarshal(item, &fix); err != nil {
197					return err
198				}
199				fix.Target = strings.TrimSpace(fix.Target)
200				fix.Fix = strings.TrimSpace(fix.Fix)

43	# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
44	# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
45	# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
46	# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
47	# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
48	frontend-verify:
49		@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
50		@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
51		missing=0; \
52		for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
53			path=".$$ref"; \
54			if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
55				echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
56				missing=$$((missing+1)); \
57			fi; \
58		done; \
59		if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
60			echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
61			exit 1; \
62		fi; \
63		echo "  ok"
64	
65	# Sync the canonical default-plugin skill tree (../library/skills/) and its
66	# manifest into internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/ so //go:embed (which
67	# cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks them up. Runs as a prereq
68	# of build/test/dev so contributors never see a stale embedded default
69	# package. rsync --delete keeps the skill destination in exact sync.
70	DEFAULT_PKG_SRC := ../library
71	DEFAULT_PKG_DST := internal/registry/defaultplugin/library
72	copy-skills:
73		@if [ ! -d "$(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills" ]; then \
74			echo "error: $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
75		fi
76		@rsync -a --delete $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills/ $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/skills/
77		@cp $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/package.yaml $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/package.yaml

91		@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
92			OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
93			ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
94			echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
95			GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \

113	test-full: copy-skills

119	# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override

121	BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)

131	# Enforce the aggregate coverage ratchet recorded in coverage-baseline.yml.

16	func compileReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) *jsonschema.Schema {
17		t.Helper()
18	
19		_, currentFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
20		require.True(t, ok)
21	
22		schemaPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(currentFile), "schema", "readiness-checks.schema.json")
23		raw, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)
24		require.NoError(t, err)
25	
26		compiler := jsonschema.NewCompiler()
27		require.NoError(t, compiler.AddResource("readiness-checks.schema.json", strings.NewReader(string(raw))))
28	
29		// Compile also asserts the schema document itself is well-formed.
30		schema, err := compiler.Compile("readiness-checks.schema.json")
31		require.NoError(t, err)
32		return schema
33	}
34	
35	// TestReadinessChecksSchema locks the readiness payload shape behind the shared
36	// readiness-checks.schema.json so the producer prompt and the Go decoder cannot
37	// drift the way the parent wedge (ddx-f9ddaa68) did, where the producer emitted
38	// readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string.
39	//
40	// Every verdict form readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON
41	// (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:91-121) accepts must both
42	//   - validate against the schema (AC1/AC4: tightening the schema to reject any
43	//     accepted form fails this test), and
44	//   - decode to the canonical readinessVerdict (AC2: pass/fail/passthrough/empty).
45	func TestReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) {
46		schema := compileReadinessChecksSchema(t)
47	
48		// buildPayload returns a full readiness payload whose single readiness_checks
49		// entry carries the given raw JSON for verdict, or omits verdict when empty.
50		buildPayload := func(verdictJSON string) string {
51			entry := `{"reason":"missing_verification","evidence":"AC lacks go test","checkable_before_attempt":true`
52			if verdictJSON != "" {
53				entry += `,"verdict":` + verdictJSON
54			}
55			entry += `}`
56			return `{"classification":"needs_refine","rationale":"check","readiness_checks":[` + entry + `]}`
57		}
58	
59		cases := []struct {
60			name        string
61			verdictJSON string
62			want        readinessVerdict
63		}{
64			{name: "bool_true_to_pass", verdictJSON: `true`, want: "pass"},
65			{name: "bool_false_to_fail", verdictJSON: `false`, want: "fail"},
66			{name: "string_fail_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"fail"`, want: "fail"},
67			{name: "string_ready_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"ready"`, want: "ready"},
68			{name: "string_not_ready_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"not_ready"`, want: "not_ready"},
69			{name: "null_empty", verdictJSON: `null`, want: ""},
70			{name: "absent_empty", verdictJSON: "", want: ""},
71		}
72	
73		for _, tc := range cases {
74			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
75				payload := buildPayload(tc.verdictJSON)
76	
77				// AC1/AC4: the schema must accept every form the decoder accepts.
78				var v any
79				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &v))
80				require.NoError(t, schema.Validate(v),
81					"verdict form %q must validate against readiness-checks.schema.json", tc.verdictJSON)
82	
83				// AC2: the same form must decode to the canonical readinessVerdict.
84				var classified preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult
85				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &classified))
86				require.Len(t, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks, 1)
87				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks[0].Verdict)
88			})
89		}
90	
91		t.Run("malformed_kind_rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
92			payload := buildPayload(`{"kind":"pass"}`)
93	
94			var v any
95			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &v))
96			require.Error(t, schema.Validate(v), "object verdicts must be rejected by readiness-checks.schema.json")
97		})
98	}

17	// TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement verifies that a
18	// replacement description can remove stale/noisy old prose when explicit
19	// commitments and durable anchors are preserved. (AC1)
20	func TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement(t *testing.T) {
21		original := &bead.Bead{
22			ID: "ddx-test0001",
23			Description: "PROBLEM\nThe old intake system duplicated stale chat noise in every bead prompt." +
24				" Extra: references to slack thread 2025-01, per internal RFC-47.\n\n" +
25				"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces append-only check\n\n" +
26				"PROPOSED FIX\nReplace substring check per FEAT-010 and ADR-023\n\n" +
27				"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n",
28			Acceptance: "1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n",
29		}
30		// Compressed replacement: drops noisy prose, keeps all explicit commitments.
31		compressedDesc := "PROBLEM\nAppend-only check rejects valid replacements per FEAT-010 and ADR-023.\n\n" +
32			"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces substring containment\n\n" +
33			"PROPOSED FIX\nReplace with commitment-based validation\n\n" +
34			"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n"
35	
36		rewrite := PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
37			Description:   compressedDesc,
38			ChangedFields: []string{"description"},
39		}
40	
41		got, _, _, preservation, err := validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite(original, rewrite)
42		require.NoError(t, err)
43		assert.Equal(t, strings.TrimSpace(compressedDesc), got.Description)
44		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "FEAT-010")
45		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "ADR-023")
46		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119")
47		assert.Contains(t, preservation, "non_scope:Do not change acceptance criteria validator")
48		// Stale prose is gone from the replacement.
49		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "slack thread 2025-01")
50		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "RFC-47")
51	}

53	// TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsExpansionReplacement verifies that a vague
54	// one-line description can expand into a standalone executable bead when the

65		// criteria — those belong in the acceptance field.

87	// items, governing artifact references, named tests, or unresolved constraints
88	// cannot be silently removed by a replacement rewrite. (AC3)

100				newDesc: "PROBLEM\nfix intake\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/foo.go:10\n",

102			},

113					"ROOT CAUSE\nmust satisfy TestPreClaimIntakeHook\n",
114				newDesc: "PROBLEM\nfix intake\n\nROOT CAUSE\nsee failing check\n",
115				wantErr: `drops commitment "TestPreClaimIntakeHook"`,
116			},
117			{
118				name: "file_line_dropped",
119				oldDesc: "PROBLEM\nfix intake\n\n" +

121				newDesc: "PROBLEM\nfix intake\n\nROOT CAUSE\nsome other cause\n",
122				wantErr: `drops commitment "cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119"`,
123			},

143	// TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence verifies that the
144	// intake-rewritten event records changed_fields, rationale, before/after
145	// hashes, and preservation_evidence sufficient to audit the original text
146	// without duplicating it in the replacement body. (AC4)
147	func TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence(t *testing.T) {
148		root := t.TempDir()
149		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName))
150		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
151	
152		oldDesc := "PROBLEM\nstale chat noise from RFC-47\n\n" +
153			"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces append-only\n\n" +
154			"PROPOSED FIX\nReplace per FEAT-010\n\n" +
155			"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n"
156		newDesc := "PROBLEM\nAppend-only check rejects valid replacements per FEAT-010.\n\n" +
157			"ROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119 enforces substring containment\n\n" +
158			"NON-SCOPE\nDo not change acceptance criteria validator\n"
159	
160		b := &bead.Bead{
161			ID:          "ddx-test0004",
162			Title:       "test: replacement evidence",
163			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
164			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
165			Priority:    1,
166			Description: oldDesc,
167			Acceptance:  "1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence\n",
168		}
169		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
170	
171		intake := PreClaimIntakeResult{
172			Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
173			Detail:  "compressed stale RFC-47 prose",
174			Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
175				Description:   newDesc,
176				ChangedFields: []string{"description"},
177			},
178		}
179		at := time.Date(2026, 5, 8, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
180		err := applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite(store, b.ID, "test-worker", intake, at)
181		require.NoError(t, err)
182	
183		events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
184		require.NoError(t, err)
185	
186		var found bool
187		for _, ev := range events {
188			if ev.Kind != "intake-rewritten" {
189				continue
190			}
191			found = true
192			var body preClaimIntakeRewriteEventBody
193			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
194	
195			// Rationale and changed fields are recorded.
196			assert.Equal(t, "compressed stale RFC-47 prose", body.Rationale)
197			assert.Equal(t, []string{"description"}, body.ChangedFields)
198	
199			// Before/after hashes are present and different.
200			assert.NotEmpty(t, body.Before.DescriptionSHA)
201			assert.NotEmpty(t, body.After.DescriptionSHA)
202			assert.NotEqual(t, body.Before.DescriptionSHA, body.After.DescriptionSHA)
203	
204			// Preservation evidence lists the durable anchors that were checked.
205			assert.Contains(t, body.PreservationEvidence, "FEAT-010")
206			assert.Contains(t, body.PreservationEvidence, "cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:119")
207			assert.Contains(t, body.PreservationEvidence, "non_scope:Do not change acceptance criteria validator")
208	
209			// Original body is NOT duplicated in the event.
210			assert.NotContains(t, ev.Body, "stale chat noise from RFC-47")
211		}
212		require.True(t, found, "intake-rewritten event must be recorded")
213	}
